articles.title: World Cup goals chart led by PSG and Real - plus Palace and Sunderland
articles.summary: Crystal Palace and Sunderland lead the Premier League’s World Cup scoring charts after the 2026 group stage, ahead of Arsenal, Liverpool and Manchester City.

articles.title: Valenzuela targets Stevenson after revenge rematch with De Los Santos
articles.summary: Jose Valenzuela has revenge on his mind when he fights Edwin De Los Santos on Sunday night, live on Sky Sports.

articles.title: Fuchs leaves Newport County after just seven months
articles.summary: Newport County are hunting a fourth manager in little more than two years ahead of the 2026-27 season.

articles.title: Is a former England striker right about reasons for Scottish football's ills?
articles.summary: From undervalued broadcasting deals to unfulfilled potential, Ian Wright says he feels sorry for Scottish football fans - but is the former England striker right?

articles.summary: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

articles.title: Ryu takes one-shot lead into final round of Women's PGA Championship
articles.summary: Haeran Ryu shot a four-under 68 on Saturday to take a one-shot lead over Brooke Henderson after three rounds of the KPMG Women's PGA Championship.

articles.title: 27 flights, 24 matches: How Fifa's president is jetting around World Cup
articles.summary: Gianni Infantino has clocked up thousands of air miles across North America so far.

articles.title: Maestro Messi scores again and breaks another record in the process
articles.summary: Lionel Messi comes off the bench to score his sixth goal of the 2026 World Cup as Argentina beat Jordan 3-1 in Dallas and becomes the first player to score in seven World Cup games in a row.

articles.title: Algeria and Austria both qualify for last 32 after dramatic injury-time goals
articles.summary: Algeria and Austria each score injury-time goals in a 3-3 draw, a result which sees both sides qualify from Group J and into the last 32 of the FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: Messi comes off the bench to score as Argentina beat Jordan
articles.summary: Lionel Messi comes off the bench to become the first player in FIFA World Cup history to score in seven consecutive matches, as Argentina defeat Jordan 3-1 in their final game in Group J.

articles.title: Kane becomes England's top World Cup scorer - and now chases other greats
articles.summary: Harry Kane became England's all-time leading World Cup scorer with his 12th goal in the win against Panama on Saturday, but how does he compare to other country's greats?

articles.title: 'Get rid of VAR!' Rooney unhappy Sanchez goal ruled out
articles.summary: BBC Sport World Cup pundit Wayne Rooney cannot believe Davinson Sanchez's goal for Colombia against Portugal was ruled out because the defender was offside by a toe.

articles.title: England labour past Panama to qualify for last 32 as group winners
articles.summary: Goals from Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane help England secure a 2-0 win over Panama, as Thomas Tuchel's side qualify for the World Cup last 32 as winners of Group L.

articles.title: 'Superstar' Bellingham inspires England to victory over Panama and top spot in group
